title: Pan Seared Japanese Celery Nabemono
description: A lazy Sunday Japanese dish featuring crispy fried celery in a savory wine sauce, served in a nabemono style hot pot. This low fat friendly recipe is perfect for a relaxing weekend meal.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a deep frying pan to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a bowl, mix together the flour and a pinch of salt.
Dredge the celery pieces in the flour mixture, shaking off excess.
Carefully add the floured celery to the hot oil and deep fry until golden brown.
Remove the fried celery from the oil and place on a paper towel lined plate to drain excess oil.
In the same pan, add the sake, mirin, and soy sauce, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Bring the sauce to a simmer and cook until it has reduced by half.
Add the dashi broth to the pan and bring to a simmer.
Add the fried celery back to the pan and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Garnish with green onions and serve hot in a nabemono style hot pot.
